duration: About 5 obsevations totalling close to one minute
description: This bird was smaller than nearby Broad-tailed and Black-chinned Hummingbirds, and about same size as Callliope. Tail was very short and similar in shape to Calliope. Interior rectrices(R-5 & 6) were especially short. Bill was thin and longer than Calliope but shorter than most female Black-chins.  Crown was greenish, auriculars grayish with broad white supercilium extending behind the eye & curving downward around grayish auriculars. Thin black eyeline extended into lores to the bill.   Back & nape were greenish; throat was solid white as was rest of underparts, except turning a dusky off-white on the flanks. There was no buff on the underparts nor any streaks on the throat.  Tail was very short and dark excepting white tips on outer rectrices.Behaviors: Bird was observed coming to Hummer feeder; fed at 5 to 10 second intervals. Actively pumped and wagged its spreaded tail while feeding & approaching feeder.Call: None heardPlumage: insure if immature or adult
